reason: Task scope, MVP boundaries, endpoint support, and out-of-scope exclusions for native Grok provider work.
Primary design for native Aether Grok provider family, auth_config fields, transport modules, model registry, and phased implementation order.
Existing Codex, Kiro, and Antigravity provider patterns to mirror for provider_type, auth_config, import, refresh, transport, and model fetch.
Provider catalog, transport snapshot, scheduler selection, and admin pool architecture that Grok must reuse.
Reference Grok non-video API surface, dynamic model visibility, image generation paths, auth materials, and upstream endpoint shape.
Decision record comparing sidecar, generic transport, and native provider-family options, with verification plan.
Security and operations guardrails for secret storage, admin payload redaction, proxy handling, and media fetch exclusions.
Provider transport ownership, public facade rules, and pre-development checklist for provider-specific request/auth/URL work.
Rules for URL, header, body, streaming, and provider hook construction when adding Grok transport behavior.
Format identity, conversion, stream rewriting, image request, and provider-private envelope boundaries.
Streaming and provider compatibility rules needed for chat, responses, messages, and image surfaces.
Gateway app-layer boundaries for routes, admin handlers, scheduler integration, and execution runtime wiring.
Proxy/control execution flow, request buffering, stream/sync paths, and candidate-loop integration rules.
Admin helper boundaries, deterministic payload shaping, redaction, and test expectations.
Provider OAuth adapter boundaries, provider_type registration, token/auth_config handling, and refresh behavior rules.
Model-fetch strategy, preset/live model handling, deterministic normalization, and gateway integration points.
Pure scheduler domain rules for candidate selection, skip reasons, quota, health, ranking, and request-candidate reports.
Repository contract boundaries for provider catalog records, key auth_config, metadata, and persistence-facing structs.
